description: A script relies on objects left in the global environment, so it fails or differs in a clean session.
body: Failure mode: a script depends on variables or loaded data lingering in the global environment from an earlier interactive session, so it breaks or produces different output when run fresh. Detection signals: code that works interactively but fails on a clean render; object not found errors only in batch; results that change after restarting R. Do not rely on interactive session state. Safer fallback: render in a fresh session, set a seed for stochastic steps, and load every dependency explicitly within the script.
